documentation.value: The value to write. Accepts `string`, `int`, `object`, etc. Overloaded for many types.

Reads the next line of characters from the standard input.
```csharp
string? input = Console.ReadLine();
```

Replaces format items `{0}`, `{1}`, etc. in a string with string representations of arguments.
```csharp
string s = String.Format("{0} is {1} years old", name, age);
string s2 = String.Format("{0:C}", 1234.56); // $1,234.56
```
A composite format string with `{index}` or `{index:format}` placeholders.
First argument to format.
Second argument to format.
Additional arguments.

Concatenates elements using the specified separator.
```csharp
string csv = String.Join(", ", new[] { "a", "b", "c" }); // "a, b, c"
string path = String.Join("/", parts);
```
The string to use as a separator.
An `IEnumerable<string>` or `string[]` of values to join.

Returns the larger of two values.
```csharp
int max = Math.Max(10, 20); // 20
double d = Math.Max(3.14, 2.71);
```
First value (`int`, `double`, `long`, `decimal`, etc.).
Second value of the same type.

Returns the smaller of two values.
```csharp
int min = Math.Min(10, 20); // 10
```
First value.
Second value of the same type.
The object to add to the list.

Removes the first occurrence of a specific object from the list.
```csharp
bool removed = list.Remove("Alice"); // true if found
```
The object to remove.
The key of the element to add.
The value of the element to add.

Converts a value to a 32-bit signed integer.
```csharp
int n = Convert.ToInt32("42");
int n2 = Convert.ToInt32(3.99); // 4 (banker's rounding)
int n3 = Convert.ToInt32(true); // 1
```
The value to convert (`string`, `double`, `bool`, `object`, etc.).
A string containing a number to convert.
An `Action` or `Func<T>` delegate to execute asynchronously.
The sequence of values to project.
A transform function `Func<TSource, TResult>` applied to each element.
The sequence to filter.
A function `Func<TSource, bool>` to test each element.
